title_en: Have Accommodations Become an Unfair Advantage in Higher Education?
title_zh: 高等教育中的“便利照顾”是否已经成为不公平的优势？

paragraphs.en: Accommodation Nation
paragraphs.zh: 《Accommodation Nation》

paragraphs.en: In the January 2026 issue, Rose Horowitch reported on American colleges’ extra-time-on-tests problem.
paragraphs.zh: 在2026年1月号中，罗斯·霍罗维奇（Rose Horowitch）报道了美国大学考试延长考试时间的乱象。

paragraphs.en: As a teacher and an administrator with more than 40 years of experience providing special-education services in public schools, I have watched the evolution of awareness and support that Rose Horowitch describes. The Americans With Disabilities Act was meant to level the playing field by removing obstacles to publicly funded services for anyone identified as disabled. It was not enacted to provide advantages. But that has begun to change.
paragraphs.zh: 作为一名在公立学校从事特殊教育服务超过40年的教师和行政人员，我亲眼见证了罗斯·霍罗维奇所描述的认知与支持的发展变化。《美国残障人士法案》最初旨在通过消除对任何被认定为残障者的公共资助服务中的障碍来实现公平竞争，并不意味着要提供优势。然而，这一点已经开始改变。

paragraphs.en: The root of the abuses, I think, is in the language of the law. The vagueness of crucial terms allows for easy manipulation of the eligibility process. The determination of a disability has become more subjective. Compassionate educators and invested parents can easily over-identify disabilities. I have seen students who perform extremely well on psychoeducational assessments and meet or exceed all grade-level standards put on an accommodation plan based on mild distractibility or slow written output.
paragraphs.zh: 我认为，滥用行为的根源在于法律用语本身。关键术语的模糊性使得资格认定流程容易被操纵。残障认定也变得更主观。心怀同情的教育者和投入的家长很容易把学生“认定得过多”。我见过一些学生，在心理教育评估中表现极其优秀，并且达到或超过所有年级水平标准，却因为轻微的注意力分散或书面输出速度慢，就被列入便利照顾计划。

paragraphs.en: Thanks to the ADA, many students who need support are seeing futures that otherwise would not have been possible. Yet the disproportionate number of “disabled” but high-achieving students at secondary schools reflects the pressure that students and their anxious parents feel to find any edge in a society where young adults face shrinking opportunities for gainful employment, secure incomes, and stable communities. These circumstances have turned a well-intentioned law into a ticket for advantages.
paragraphs.zh: 由于《美国残障人士法案》（ADA），许多需要支持的学生看到了过去本来无法实现的前景。然而，许多中学里“残障”但成绩优秀的学生数量明显偏高，反映了学生及其焦虑父母为了在一个年轻人就业机会减少、稳定收入和社区生活难以保障的社会里获得任何优势而承受的压力。这些环境压力把本是善意的法律，变成了获取优势的“通行证”。

paragraphs.en: Patty Bell Deerfield, Mass.
paragraphs.zh: 帕蒂·贝尔·德尔菲尔德，马萨诸塞州

paragraphs.en: I recently graduated from law school in Canada, where the accommodations framework is very similar to that of the United States. I can confirm that the issue is quietly but frequently discussed among students. Because many law schools apply a curved grading system, students without time-based accommodations sometimes feel that attaining an A is virtually impossible. Numerous students conveyed to me a sense of disillusionment: No matter how diligently they prepare, they may still receive a B+ simply because the A-range grades have been taken by those who had twice the time to complete the assessment.
paragraphs.zh: 我最近毕业于加拿大的一所法学院，那里的便利安排体系与美国非常相似。我可以确认，这个问题在学生中虽然不高调却相当常见地被讨论。由于许多法学院实行曲线评分制度，未获得时间延长便利的学生有时会觉得，想拿到A几乎不可能。许多学生向我表达过一种幻灭感：无论他们多么认真准备，最终也可能只得到B+，因为A档成绩已经被那些在完成考试时拥有两倍时间的学生占据了。

paragraphs.en: In response, some of my peers sought accommodations themselves—sometimes out of legitimate need, but sometimes because of the competitive pressure created by the system. This situation warrants careful reexamination. Short of eliminating accommodations—which would be contrary to human-rights principles and entirely inappropriate—the most constructive solution may be to reconsider the role of time limits in assessments altogether. Providing all students with a reasonable and uniform timeframe in which to demonstrate their knowledge, without excessive time pressure, may help restore both fairness and clarity in evaluation.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，一些同学主动申请了便利安排——有时出于真实需求，但也有时是因为体系制造的竞争压力所致。这样的情况值得慎重重新审视。在不取消便利安排的前提下——这既违反人权原则，也完全不妥——最具建设性的解决方案，或许是重新思考评估中时间限制的作用。给所有学生提供一个合理且统一的时间框架来展示其知识，在不过度施加时间压力的情况下，或许有助于恢复评估中的公平性与清晰性。

paragraphs.en: David Kantrovitz Maple, Ontario, Canada
paragraphs.zh: David Kantrovitz Maple，加拿大安大略省

paragraphs.en: I am a former college disability-services professional who worked for more than 24 years in both two- and four-year institutions. Before entering the field professionally, I myself was a student who used accommodations in the early and mid-1990s. Although it is absolutely correct that the 2008 ADA amendments, the expanded assessment guidance, and the dramatic rise in ADHD, anxiety, and depression diagnoses among young people have all contributed to the increase in students receiving accommodations, so, too, has the corporatization of higher education.
paragraphs.zh: 我是一名前高校残障服务从业者，在两年制和四年制院校都工作过超过24年。进入这一行业之前，我本人也在1990年代早期和中期作为学生使用过学习便利安排。2008年《美国残障人士法》修正案、扩大的评估指引，以及青少年群体中ADHD、焦虑和抑郁诊断率的显著上升，确实都促成了获得便利安排学生人数的增加；同样，推动这一变化的，还有高等教育的“企业化”趋势。

paragraphs.en: Today, universities treat their students as customers. Rising tuition costs have forced administrators to adopt a customer-service model, which in turn has heightened pressure on staff to keep students and their families satisfied. Academic accommodations are often folded into the model, and disability-services providers are encouraged to waive or dilute documentation standards. This can improve access for students who lack financial resources, but my experience has been that the students who benefit the most are those with means. Demanding parents expect institutions to remove every obstacle to their children’s ideal college experience. This environment makes it extremely difficult for disability-services professionals to apply consistent standards or to make thoughtful, evidence-based decisions about which academic adjustments are genuinely warranted and which are simply desired.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，大学把学生当作客户来看待。不断上升的学费迫使学校管理层采用客户服务模式，这又加大了工作人员向学生及其家庭提供满意服务的压力。学术便利安排常被纳入这种服务框架，残障服务人员也被鼓励放宽或削弱证明文件的门槛。这确实可以改善缺乏经济资源学生的入学机会，但根据我的经验，受益最多的通常是那些本身有经济实力的学生。要求严格的家长希望学校消除一切阻碍，让自己的孩子拥有理想的大学体验。这样的环境使残障服务专业人员几乎无法始终如一地执行统一标准，也难以基于证据做出深思熟虑的判断——哪些学术调整是真正需要的，哪些只是被希望得到的。

paragraphs.en: Chris Kinney Crozet, Va.
paragraphs.zh: Chris Kinney，弗吉尼亚州Crozet

paragraphs.en: I was born with optic atrophy and am just blind enough to be designated “legally blind.” In 1961, I was completing a master’s degree at the University of Chicago; I needed only to pass a language exam to graduate. These exams were given in a huge room in an old stone building with little windows up near high ceilings. I didn’t have enough light to read. I failed. I went from one office to another requesting permission to take the test again with a lamp. No one seemed to have the authority to grant my “outrageous” request. I finally found a dean who laughed with me at the absurdity. He arranged for me to retake the exam with a good reading lamp. I thanked him. I took the exam again, and I passed.
paragraphs.zh: 我出生时患有视神经萎缩，视力刚好到达“法律上失明”的程度。1961年，我在芝加哥大学攻读硕士学位，距离毕业只差通过一场语言考试。这些考试是在一座老石建筑里一间巨大且天花板很高的房间里进行的，我那里几乎没有足够的光线可用来阅读，于是我没有通过。于是我挨家挨户、一个办公室一个办公室地申请，请求允许我在有台灯的情况下重考。似乎没有任何人有权力同意我这个“荒谬”的要求。最后，我找到了一位院长，他和我一起笑着对这件事的荒唐表示理解。他帮我安排了一个带着读书台灯的补考。我向他道了谢。我又考了一次，最终通过了。

paragraphs.en: Naomi Woronov New York, N.Y.
paragraphs.zh: 作者：Naomi Woronov，纽约州纽约市

paragraphs.en: I am a professor at a nonselective public university who attended an elite university with accommodations for both physical and learning disabilities. I have students who would really benefit from accommodations but do not seek them. Many of these students have been raised in households and communities that stigmatize disability or seeking help.
paragraphs.zh: 我是一所非择优录取制公立大学的教授，自己曾就读于一所精英大学，并在那里得到了身体和学习障碍方面的便利安排。我的一些学生其实真的会从便利安排中受益，但他们并没有去申请。许多这样的学生来自家庭和社区，这些环境里把残障或寻求帮助视为一种耻辱。

paragraphs.en: The Americans With Disabilities Act exists in an inequitable context—so of course economic inequality can hamper its application. But making accommodations harder to obtain is the wrong solution. Perhaps we ought to consider a more radical approach: rethinking pedagogies to be more inclusive so that accommodations are rendered moot. Unless the learning objective of a course is specifically to do something quickly, wouldn’t it be better if everyone had the time and means to demonstrate their mastery of a new concept or skill? We don’t lower academic rigor by making accommodations universal—we give students the ability to fully demonstrate what they’ve learned.
paragraphs.zh: 《美国残障人士法案》本身就在一个不公平的背景下运作，因此经济不平等当然会妨碍它的落实。但让便利安排更难获得，这并不是正确的解决办法。也许我们该考虑一种更激进的做法：重新思考教学法，让课堂更具包容性，从而让便利安排本身变得多余。除非某门课程的学习目标本身就是要“做事很快”，否则是否更好的是，让每个人都有时间和手段去展示自己对新概念或新技能的掌握？我们让便利安排普及，并不会降低学术标准；我们只是让学生有能力充分展示他们学到的内容。

paragraphs.en: Miranda Worthen Professor, San José State University San José, Calif.
paragraphs.zh: Miranda Worthen，加利福尼亚州圣何塞市圣何塞州立大学教授

paragraphs.en: Why not allow extended time for all students? In a world where AI encourages quick fixes and discourages independent thinking, aren’t critical and deep thinking exactly what we want to cultivate at a university? My guess is that most students will not stay one minute more than they have to in an exam room.
paragraphs.zh: 为什么不让所有学生都可以延长考试时间呢？在一个鼓励速成并且削弱独立思考能力、让AI参与作答的时代，难道我们在大学里最希望培养的不是批判性和深度思考吗？我猜大多数学生在考场里宁可多花一分钟也不会待在里面。

paragraphs.en: Julie F. Skolnick Potomac, Md.
paragraphs.zh: Julie F. Skolnick，马里兰州波托马克

paragraphs.en: Rose Horowitch replies:
paragraphs.zh: Rose Horowitch 回应：

paragraphs.en: Thank you for engaging with my article so thoughtfully. A perfect structure would allow accommodations for every student who needs them while preventing students who don’t from gaining an unfair advantage. But I understand that the same factors that allow more privileged students to access accommodations—wealth, cultural capital—can prevent others from getting the support they need. The suggestion to remove time limits is an interesting one. Some professors I spoke with said that they, too, favored this solution—they didn’t see any pedagogical need for their students to complete tests in a certain amount of time. The idea would create new demands at universities for physical space and proctors, but it could be worth exploring.
paragraphs.zh: 感谢你们如此认真地与我的文章互动。一个理想的制度应该能让每一位需要的人都获得便利支持，同时阻止那些不需要的人借此获得不公平的优势。但我理解，正是让更有特权的学生更容易拿到便利支持的同样因素——财富、文化资本——也会让其他人无法获得他们真正需要的帮助。取消考试时限的建议很有意思。我与一些教授交流过，他们也倾向于这个方案——他们也不认为教学上有必要要求学生在固定时间内完成考试。这个想法会让大学在教学空间和监考人员方面承担新的压力，但值得认真研究。

paragraphs.en: In this month’s cover story, “I Found It: The Best Free Restaurant Bread in America,” Caity Weaver travels across the country to determine which establishment—from strip-mall chains to upscale brasseries—offers its customers the greatest complimentary bread. Armed with more than 500 nominations, Weaver eventually crowned just one loaf champion. To accompany the story, we gathered 18 of the breads Weaver sampled for a photo shoot. On the cover are slices of a rustic country loaf from the three-Michelin-star Joël Robuchon at the MGM Grand in Las Vegas. (This is not the best free restaurant bread in America.)
paragraphs.zh: 在本月封面故事《我找到了：美国最好的免费餐厅面包》中，Caity Weaver 横跨全国考察了各类餐厅——从郊区商场连锁店到高端酒馆——找出哪个场所给顾客提供的免费面包最美味。她共收集了500多份提名，最终只为一个面包下了“冠军”称号。为配合这篇报道，我们收集了 Weaver 试吃的18种面包并拍了照片。封面上是拉斯维加斯梅姬德·格兰德（MGM Grand）三颗米其林星级餐厅乔尔·鲁布雄（Joël Robuchon）的乡村乡土面包切片。（这不是美国最好的免费餐厅面包。）

paragraphs.en: — Bifen Xu, Senior Photo Editor
paragraphs.zh: — Xu Bifen，资深摄影编辑

paragraphs.en: “The Women of Avenger Field” (April) stated that Patricia Perry moved two hours southeast to study political science at UC Berkeley and that she arrived in Sweetwater, Texas, on July 5, 1943. In fact, she moved two hours southwest, and she began training in Sweetwater on that date. The article also included a quote attributed to a base commander that was in fact a quote from another source paraphrasing his remarks, and used an incorrect verb tense in a quote from Pete Hegseth. “The Secrets of Indigenous Art” (March) stated that a traveling exhibition of Native abstraction was organized by Jeffrey Gibson. In fact, it was co-organized by Gibson and Jenelle Porter. “How America Got So Sick” (March) implied that tuberculosis was among the diseases covered by the national vaccine schedule for which cases are surging. In fact, the United States does not routinely vaccinate for tuberculosis.
paragraphs.zh: 《复仇者空军基地的女性》（4月）写道，帕特里夏·佩里（Patricia Perry）从西南偏西约两小时车程搬到加州大学伯克利分校学习政治学，并于1943年7月5日抵达德州苏厄特沃特（Sweetwater）。实际上，她是向西南偏南约两小时车程前往，并且她在同日开始在苏厄特沃特接受训练。文章还引用了一段基地司令的话，但那其实是另一位消息源转述他言论时的说法；同时还把彼得·赫格塞思（Pete Hegseth）的一段引语中的动词时态用错了。 《原住民艺术的秘密》（3月）写道，一场关于原住民抽象艺术的巡回展览由杰弗里·吉布森（Jeffrey Gibson）组织。事实上，该展览是吉布森与詹妮尔·波特（Jenelle Porter）联合主办的。 《美国为何变得如此“病态”》（3月）暗示结核病是美国儿童国家免疫计划中正在“激增”且需要常规接种的疾病之一。实际上，美国并不对结核病实施常规疫苗接种。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the May 2026 print edition with the headline “The Commons.”
paragraphs.zh: 本文发表于2026年5月印刷版，标题为“THE COMMONS”。
